kept.text:
$SOFI (Max Pain $17 | Price $16.43) — May 8 expiry

1.3M in calls at $35 and price is 57 cents under the pin. The structure is loaded on both sides but the put wall underneath is the real story.

Puts run heavy from $1 all the way through $14 — nearly 900k at the low strikes, still meaningful through $15. Calls are quiet until $19, build steadily from $20, then the staircase turns steep from $28 to $35. 

Clear $17 and positioning gravity pulls toward $20 where the call side starts engaging. Slip below $16 and there's no support until the put wall catches at $14.

🎯 Coiled: $17 is the line, $20 is where the call side engages, $35 is the ceiling at 1.3M.

quoted_tweet.text:
$SOFI (weekly) — Second rejection at the EMA cloud. Bears are still very present there and not backing down just yet.

The pullback is technically a higher low, but barely.

The playing field is pretty clear though:

> $15 needs to hold as support
> $19 needs to be cleared to break free

kept.text:
The market still does not understand $SOFI.

That is exactly why I continue to buy and hold for the long term.

Most people still analyze SoFi like it is just an online lender. I think that is the biggest mistake you can make with this company.

SoFi is becoming a full financial ecosystem, and this quarter showed exactly why I believe the long-term thesis is still getting stronger.
